name: Claude Managed Agents + ant CLI
summary: public beta for building and deploying Claude agents — sandboxed execution, checkpointing, scoped permissions, SSE streaming; new ant CLI lets you ship agents from the terminal

name: Meta Muse Spark
summary: Meta's first closed-source model — voice/text/image input, live on meta.ai now. Can generate minigames, excels at visual STEM, rolling out to WhatsApp and Ray-Ban glasses

name: GLM-5.1
summary: MIT-licensed open 744B MoE model (40B active, 200K context) — topped SWE-Bench for coding, first open model to beat all closed rivals on real-world code; weights on Hugging Face

name: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
summary: open-source, production-ready agent framework for Python and .NET — unifies Semantic Kernel + AutoGen with graph-based multi-agent workflows, MCP interop, and a stable LTS API

name: Claude Cowork + Dispatch
summary: Claude desktop control now on Windows — clicks, types, opens apps autonomously. Dispatch lets you hand off tasks from your phone while Claude works on your computer

name: Cursor 3
summary: rebuilt IDE around parallel AI agents — run many agents at once across local, cloud, worktree, and remote SSH; new Design Mode lets you annotate UI elements directly in the browser

name: Google Gemma 4
summary: four open-weight models (2B–31B) with Apache 2.0 license — runs on a Raspberry Pi or Mac Mini, built from Gemini 3 research, most permissive Gemma release yet

name: Veo 3.1 Lite
summary: accessible text-to-video and image-to-video model — Veo 3.1 Fast pricing slashed to open up developer access for solo creators and small teams

name: Llama 4 Scout + Maverick
summary: Meta's first natively multimodal open models — Scout (17B active/109B total MoE, runs on a single H100) and Maverick (17B active/400B total, beats GPT-4o on most benchmarks); video and image understanding, Apache 2.0

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
summary: Google's best model at release — extended thinking mode, 1M context, topped LMArena leaderboard above GPT-4.5 and Claude 3.7; experimental preview only at launch

name: GPT-4.5
summary: OpenAI's largest pre-reasoning model — wider world knowledge, improved emotional intelligence, more natural multi-turn conversation; no chain-of-thought, best for open-ended tasks and EQ-heavy use cases

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
summary: first Claude with extended thinking — toggle a deep reasoning mode that thinks for up to 128K tokens before responding; 70.3% on SWE-bench Verified, best Anthropic coding model at the time

name: o3-mini
summary: OpenAI's smallest reasoning model — three effort levels (low/medium/high), outperforms o1 on math and coding at a fraction of the cost; fastest path to reasoning for high-volume tasks

name: DeepSeek R1
summary: open-source reasoning model matching o1 performance — fully MIT-licensed weights; Chinese lab releases 671B MoE with 37B active params, trained for a fraction of GPT-4's cost; shocked the industry

name: DeepSeek V3
summary: 671B MoE model open-sourced with MIT license — 37B active parameters, outperforms Llama 3.1 405B and Qwen 2.5 72B on coding and math; trained for ~$6M total, challenging frontier lab economics

name: Gemini 2.0 Flash
summary: Google's next-gen workhorse — 1M context, native audio and image output (not just input), significantly faster and cheaper than Gemini 1.5 Pro; first model in the Gemini 2.0 family

name: Claude 3.5 Haiku
summary: fastest and cheapest Claude 3.5 — matches Claude 3 Opus on coding benchmarks at Claude 3 Haiku pricing; ideal for high-volume tasks, agents, and real-time applications

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet + computer use
summary: improved Sonnet with new computer use beta — Claude can see your screen, move a cursor, click, and type autonomously; 49% on OSWorld desktop benchmark; first frontier model with native desktop control

name: o1
summary: OpenAI's first reasoning model — chains internal thinking steps before responding, PhD-level science and math, top 500 on US Math Olympiad; much slower and costlier than GPT-4o but dramatically better at hard problems

name: Mistral Large 2
summary: Mistral's 123B flagship — outperforms Llama 3.1 70B and matches GPT-4o on coding and reasoning; natively multilingual (dozen+ languages), function calling, Apache 2.0 weights available

name: Llama 3.1
summary: Meta's 405B open-weights flagship — matches GPT-4o on many benchmarks with Apache 2.0 license; 8B and 70B variants also updated; 128K context across all sizes; largest openly licensed model at launch

name: GPT-4o mini
summary: cheap, capable small model replacing GPT-3.5 Turbo — 82% MMLU, vision support, 128K context; aimed at high-volume embedded use cases at a fraction of GPT-4o's cost

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet
summary: Anthropic's first 3.5-tier model — outperforms Claude 3 Opus on coding, reasoning, and vision at Sonnet pricing; Artifacts feature added to claude.ai for in-chat document and code editing

name: GPT-4o
summary: omni multimodal model with real-time audio and video — natively processes voice with emotional tone detection, 2x faster and 50% cheaper than GPT-4 Turbo API, free tier access via ChatGPT

name: Llama 3
summary: Meta's open-source model family — 8B and 70B variants; 70B outperforms Gemini 1.5 Pro and Mistral Medium on coding and reasoning benchmarks; most capable openly available model at the time

name: Claude 3 family
summary: three-tier model launch: Haiku (fastest), Sonnet (balanced), Opus (most capable) — Opus outscored GPT-4 on MMLU, HumanEval, and MATH; first Claude models with vision input across all tiers

name: Sora
summary: text-to-video model generating up to 60-second photorealistic clips with consistent characters and physics — research preview only, shown to select filmmakers and safety testers; set a new bar for generative video

name: Gemini 1.5 Pro
summary: 1M token context window — processes hour-long videos, full codebases, or books in a single context; MoE architecture rivals Gemini 1.0 Ultra quality at lower compute cost; limited preview at launch
